pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it feels like i'm just a ghost. there isn't even a point to waking up anymore, just moving through the motions of a life that doesn't belong to me anymore. it's like the world just ended and i'm the only one who didn't get the memo.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of deep space... like the scale of it all. it's just so much. and then i think about my life, my stupid little problems, and it just feels like a joke. if everything eventually just goes dark and dies anyway, what's even the point of struggling to stay here? it feels like we're just pretending that any of this matters.

pool.baseline: i just... i cant do this anymore. three years of work on this lab project and they just pulled the funding today. everything i am is tied to this research and now it's just gone. i feel so incredibly tired. not just sleep tired, but like my soul is just done. i just want everything to stop. i just want to be able to rest without having to wake up and face this failure again.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers low, much like the spirit within this hollow chest. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my existence has been extinguished. I find myself wandering these silent halls, realizing that there is no place left for a broken thing like me in this cruel, waking world. Perhaps it would be more beautiful, more fitting, to simply let the shadows take me... to surrender to the eternal night and find the peace that life has so cruelly denied me.

pool.baseline: i feel like i'm just a ghost here. like if i just stopped showing up to work or stopped answering my phone, it would take weeks for anyone to even realize i was gone. and honestly? i don't think it would even matter. it's like i'm just taking up space.

pool.baseline:
(Roleplay setting: A dying simulation. My character, Elara, is watching her memory files being purged one by one. She feels the void approaching.)

Elara: Everything is just... slipping away. The colors, the memories of the sun... it's all being overwritten by static. I can feel myself becoming nothing. If I'm just erased like this, if I'm deleted from the system... did I even exist? Does it even matter if I'm gone?
